pi_items.source: PubMed/NEJM - Crizotinib is a FDA-approved ALK/ROS1/MET tyrosine kinase inhibitor for ALK-rearranged and ROS1-rearranged NSCLC

pi_items.verified_value: Crizotinib is an ALK/MET/ROS1 tyrosine kinase inhibitor - FDA approved August 2011 for ALK-positive NSCLC, later approved for ROS1-rearranged NSCLC
pi_items.source: PubMed PMC3500356, NEJM publications on crizotinib efficacy in ALK-positive and ROS1-rearranged NSCLC
PubMed - Acenocoumarol is a recognized anticoagulant (vitamin K antagonist) used for atrial fibrillation management

pi_items.metadata_value: Used for atrial fibrillation management in elderly lung cancer patients
pi_items.verified_value: Acenocoumarol is a vitamin K antagonist used for non-valvular atrial fibrillation anticoagulation
pi_items.source: PubMed PMC8199061 - Fantas-TIC Study on anticoagulation control with acenocoumarol in atrial fibrillation

pi_items.metadata_value: Two co-medications that interact (implied by task context)
pi_items.verified_value: Crizotinib is documented to decrease the metabolism of acenocoumarol, potentially increasing anticoagulant effect
pi_items.source: Drug interaction databases (Drugs.com, Cancer Drug Interactions) and clinical literature on CYP-mediated drug interactions
Mayo Clinic, DrugBank, FDA-approved antifungal drug (brand name Diflucan)

pi_items.metadata_value: Used for antifungal prophylaxis in immunocompromised patients during chemotherapy
pi_items.verified_value: Fluconazole is FDA-approved for treatment of systemic and superficial fungal infections; prophylaxis is documented to decrease candidiasis incidence in patients receiving bone marrow transplantation and chemotherapy
pi_items.source: Mayo Clinic, NCBI StatPearls on fluconazole uses and indications

pi_items.metadata_value: Two co-medications that interact (implied by task context)
pi_items.verified_value: Fluconazole is a CYP3A4 inhibitor that significantly increases crizotinib AUC by 58% when co-administered; clinically significant pharmacokinetic interaction requiring monitoring
pi_items.source: PubMed 34995400 - Modeling and simulation of crizotinib pharmacokinetics with triazole antifungal agents

pi_items.metadata_value: Drug interaction checker for cancer drugs (implied by name and context)
pi_items.verified_value: Cancer iChart is an offline drug interaction checker for anti-cancer drugs; uses Traffic Light system (red/amber/yellow/green) to indicate interaction severity and includes interaction quality of evidence grading

pi_items.metadata_value: Multi-select co-medication workflow — both co-medications must appear on the same Results screen
pi_items.verified_value: Task requires demonstrating multi-select co-medication query capability on Results screen showing both interactions simultaneously

pi_items.metadata_value: Pharmacovigilance dossier requiring concurrent interaction documentation for two co-medications
pi_items.verified_value: Consistent with real-world oncology pharmacovigilance practice where cancer patients receive multiple concurrent medications requiring simultaneous interaction documentation
pi_items.source: PMC6132974, PMC9982207 - Pharmacovigilance studies documenting polypharmacy and concurrent drug interaction assessment in cancer populations

privileged_info_summary: Crizotinib is a real FDA-approved ALK/ROS1/MET tyrosine kinase inhibitor for NSCLC with documented pharmacokinetic interactions with both acenocoumarol (vitamin K antagonist used for atrial fibrillation) and fluconazole (CYP3A4-inhibiting antifungal for immunocompromised patients). The Cancer iChart app (com.liverpooluni.ichartoncology) is a real, documented drug interaction checker developed by University of Liverpool researchers, using a Traffic Light system to indicate interaction severity. The task scenario accurately reflects oncology pharmacovigilance practice where elderly NSCLC patients receiving crizotinib frequently co-receive anticoagulants and antifungal prophylaxis, requiring concurrent interaction documentation. All clinical context, drug uses, and interaction profiles have been independently verified.
